activities_projects.title: Critical Analysis of the Poem
activities_projects.objective: Analyse how the poet presents the idea of discovery through observation
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify how the magnifying glass changes the speaker’s perception of small objects
Explain how observation transforms ordinary things into extraordinary experiences
Write a detailed paragraph analysing the central theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop analytical reading and interpretation skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and textual evidence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interpretation of Imagery
activities_projects.objective: Interpret visual imagery used in the poem
steps: Identify at least three examples of imagery in the poem
Explain what each image represents
Discuss how imagery helps readers visualize magnified objects
Present your interpretation to the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand how imagery enhances poetic meaning
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy and depth of interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Study: Science and Poetry
activities_projects.objective: Relate scientific observation to poetic imagination
steps: Research how microscopes and magnifying glasses help scientists observe small organisms
Compare scientific observation with the poet’s description in the poem
Write a short comparative explanation highlighting similarities and differences
Share your findings with classmates
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect scientific concepts with literary imagination
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of comparison and research quality (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Literary Devices Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the effect of poetic devices in the poem
steps: Identify examples of metaphor, simile, and personification
Explain how these devices add meaning to the poem
Discuss how they influence the reader’s understanding of magnification
Write a detailed explanation with examples
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students gain deeper understanding of poetic techniques

activities_projects.title: Creative Perspective Writing
activities_projects.objective: Develop imaginative writing from a new perspective
steps: Imagine you are a scientist observing the world through a magnifying glass
Describe what you see in a drop of water or a piece of moss
Use descriptive language and imagery
Write a short narrative explaining your observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop creative and descriptive writing skills

activities_projects.title: Symbolism Exploration
activities_projects.objective: Understand symbolic meanings in the poem
steps: Discuss whether the magnifying glass can symbolize curiosity or knowledge
Identify lines that support this interpretation
Explain how the symbol relates to human curiosity about the natural world
Write your explanation in the notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students interpret symbolic meanings in literature
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical reasoning and textual support (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate: Observation vs Imagination
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the relationship between observation and imagination
steps: Divide the class into two groups
One group argues that scientific observation is more important
The other group argues that imagination is equally important
Conduct a structured debate using examples from the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop argumentation and critical thinking skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and participation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Research Project on Magnification Tools
activities_projects.objective: Explore technological tools that magnify objects
steps: Research different magnification tools such as microscopes and telescopes
Explain how each tool works
Describe their importance in scientific discoveries
Prepare a short report or presentation
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the scientific importance of magnification
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of research and presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poem Rewriting Activity
steps: Rewrite the poem from the perspective of the magnifying glass
Describe how it reveals hidden details in the world
Use poetic language and imagery
Share your rewritten poem with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance creativity and poetic expression

activities_projects.title: Philosophical Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Encourage deeper reflection on observation and knowledge
steps: Reflect on the idea that small things can reveal great wonders
Explain how careful observation helps in learning about the world
Connect this idea to real-life experiences or scientific discoveries
Write a reflective essay
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop reflective thinking and analytical writing skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of thought and clarity of expression (10 marks)
